insights.insight:
(Example insight — synthetic demo data. Replace with your own via CLAUDE.md.)

Lipids are trending favorably over three draws: LDL 3.4 → 2.9 mmol/L, triglycerides 1.4 → 1.1, HDL nudging up to 1.4. Total cholesterol 4.9 mmol/L. The residual lever is ApoB (see Cardiovascular Risk) — the particle-count marker that tracks atherogenic risk better than LDL-C alone.
insights.supplements:
• Omega-3 (EPA/DHA) 1–2 g/day — lowers triglycerides.
• Consider dietary fiber (psyllium 5–10 g/day) — modest LDL reduction.
insights.diet: Mediterranean pattern: olive oil, oily fish 2×/week, legumes, nuts. Limit refined carbs and saturated fat; both move triglycerides and LDL.
insights.activity: 150 min/week Zone-2 cardio + 2 resistance sessions raises HDL and improves particle size.
insights.checkup_schedule: Recheck full lipid panel + ApoB every 6–12 months while intervening.

insights.insight:
(Example insight — synthetic demo data. Replace with your own via CLAUDE.md.)

ApoB 0.82 g/L is just above the 0.80 optimal ceiling but falling (0.95 → 0.82). hs-CRP has normalized to 0.9 mg/L (below the 1.0 optimal line). Lp(a) 45 nmol/L is moderate and genetically fixed — it raises baseline risk independent of LDL.
insights.insight_dna: APOE ε3/ε4 carries one ε4 allele: expect higher LDL/ApoB and a stronger response to saturated-fat reduction. NOS3 and 9p21 status (if present in your DNA import) further shape endothelial and coronary risk. This is a lean, not a diagnosis.
insights.supplements:
• Continue omega-3.
• If ApoB stays >0.8 despite diet, discuss a statin with your clinician (SLCO1B1 genotype informs statin choice — see Drug Metabolism).
insights.diet: Prioritize saturated-fat reduction — ε4 carriers are especially diet-responsive.
insights.activity: Regular aerobic exercise improves endothelial (NO-mediated) function.
insights.lifestyle: No smoking; manage blood pressure — both compound genetic CV risk.
insights.checkup_schedule: ApoB + hs-CRP every 6 months; one-time Lp(a) is sufficient (it's stable for life). Consider a coronary artery calcium (CAC) scan for risk restratification.
insights.concordance:
APOE ε4 → higher ApoB|elevated ApoB|ApoB 0.82 g/L, trending down|PARTIAL
Lp(a) genetic elevation → added CV risk|higher baseline risk|Lp(a) 45 nmol/L (moderate)|CONFIRMS
Inflammatory risk|elevated hs-CRP|hs-CRP normalized to 0.9 mg/L|FAVORABLE

insights.insight:
(Example insight — synthetic demo data. Replace with your own via CLAUDE.md.)

Pharmacogenomic genotypes flag how you metabolize common drugs. Review these with any prescriber before starting a new medication — they change drug choice and dose, not just risk.
insights.insight_dna: CYP2C19 *1/*2 (intermediate) — reduced clopidogrel activation; consider an alternative antiplatelet if indicated. SLCO1B1 normal (*1/*1) — standard statin tolerance expected. Always confirm with clinical PGx testing before acting.
insights.checkup_schedule: No routine labs — this is a fixed genetic reference. Revisit when a new prescription is considered.

insights.insight:
(Example insight — synthetic demo data. Replace with your own via CLAUDE.md.)

Metabolic and inflammatory markers are in good shape (HbA1c 5.2%, hs-CRP 0.9, fasting insulin 8 µU/mL), which are the biomarkers that most move biological age. Vitamin D has climbed into the optimal band (85 nmol/L) and homocysteine is approaching its 8 µmol/L target.
insights.insight_dna: APOE ε3/ε4 (one ε4 allele) and FOXO3 rs2802292 G/T (one longevity allele) are the two headline longevity variants here. ε4 modestly raises Alzheimer's/CV risk; the FOXO3 G allele is centenarian-associated. Common-variant longevity effects are individually small — read as a lean, not a verdict.
insights.checkup_schedule: Annual metabolic + inflammatory panel; these drive most of the modifiable aging signal.

variants.relevance: ε3/ε4 — one ε4 allele: higher LDL, cardiovascular and Alzheimer's risk
One copy of the longevity-associated G allele
~65% MTHFR activity — modest homocysteine effect; ensure adequate folate/B12
Lactase non-persistent — likely lactose intolerant in adulthood
Intermediate metabolizer — reduced activation of clopidogrel
Normal transport — standard statin tolerance expected
H63D carrier — mild iron-loading potential; watch ferritin/transferrin saturation

unified_protocol:
═══ EXAMPLE MASTER PROTOCOL ═══

This is synthetic demo content. Your Claude regenerates it from your own labs,
variants, and stack — see CLAUDE.md ("Synthesizing the master protocol").

═══ A. SUPPLEMENTS ═══

─── Tier 1 (evidence-backed, targeted) ───
• Omega-3 (EPA/DHA) 1–2 g/day — triglycerides 1.1 mmol/L, cardiovascular support.
• Vitamin D3 2000 IU/day — 25-OH-D 85 nmol/L (in optimal band); maintain, don't exceed.

─── Tier 2 (supportive) ───
• Magnesium glycinate 200–400 mg/day — sleep, glucose handling.
• Methylfolate + B12 — MTHFR C677T heterozygous; keeps homocysteine ≤8 µmol/L.

═══ B. DIET ═══
Mediterranean pattern; saturated-fat reduction prioritized (APOE ε4). Lactose-free
dairy or lactase enzyme (MCM6 non-persistent).

═══ C. ACTIVITY ═══
150 min/week Zone-2 cardio + 2 resistance sessions.

═══ D. LIFESTYLE ═══
7–9 h sleep; no smoking; alcohol moderation (also lowers triglycerides).

═══ E. MONITORING ═══
ApoB + hs-CRP every 6 months; full lipids + metabolic panel annually; one-time Lp(a).

═══ H. PHARMACOGENOMICS ═══
CYP2C19 intermediate — flag before clopidogrel. SLCO1B1 normal — statins standard.
